page: UpCloud PREMIUM-80xCPU-512GB is a high-capacity virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with an x86_64 architecture, offering 80 physical cores and 80 vCPUs with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, translating to 6.4 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 500 GB SSD. The server does not include any GPU accelerators.

In benchmark evaluations, this server demonstrates exceptional multi-core and cryptographic capabilities. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU score both rank in the top tier (top 10%), supported by strong single-core performance. Cryptographic execution is highly efficient, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is also highly competitive, with small-block cached reads reaching 9301.72 GB/sec (top 10%) and large-block uncached reads at 262.5 GB/sec (top 25%). However, PassMark memory scores sit in the average tier, and LLM text generation speeds are relatively weak, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers a highly qualitative balance of massive core counts and substantial memory capacity, making it cost-efficient for workloads that scale horizontally across many physical cores without requiring dedicated GPU acceleration. It is highly suited for multi-threaded decompression tasks, database operations, and high-throughput static web serving, as evidenced by its top-tier Redis SET performance of 124.66 million operations per second and strong database benchmark results.
description: The UpCloud PREMIUM-80xCPU-512GB is a KVM-based virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor. It delivers 80 physical cores (80 vCPUs) running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of RAM and a local 500 GB SSD. Lacking a GPU, this server relies on its x86_64 architecture and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional cryptographic capabilities, and outstanding multi-threaded decompression speeds. While memory bandwidth is strong, LLM text generation remains a bottleneck due to the absence of hardware accelerators. This server is highly cost-efficient for resource-dense, non-GPU workloads. It is best suited for high-throughput database operations, Redis deployments, large-scale static web serving, and parallelized data processing tasks.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-80xCPU-512GB is a KVM virtual server featuring 80 AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB RAM, and a 500 GB SSD. It delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and cryptographic performance for demanding database workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-80xCPU-512GB is a KVM virtual server with 80 AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB RAM, and a 500 GB SSD, built for high-throughput database workloads.
tagline: A high-core, memory-dense virtual server delivering top-tier multi-threaded CPU performance and rapid database execution without GPU overhead.
bullet_points: Powered by an 80-core AMD EPYC 9575F processor with x86_64 architecture
Equipped with 512 GB of RAM and a bundled 500 GB local SSD
Top-tier multi-core CPU, cryptography, and multi-threaded decompression performance
Delivers 124.66 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Optimized for high-throughput databases, static web serving, and parallel processing
